title: SELECT, Projection, Expressions, Types, and Result Contracts · Data Contract Brief: Type Conversion
summary: An operations analyst needs a readable, reproducible ticket extract whose columns carry explicit meaning. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sql type conversion increment in a typed ticket projection query pack. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish implicit affinity from explicit conversion and test malformed or lossy values.
Build arithmetic, text, date, and CASE expressions while tracing input and output meaning.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Return unresolved high-or-critical tickets plus unowned work from the seeded database. Expose ticket_id, a readable owner label, age bucket 4, and deterministic severity/opened ordering; then test a NULL owner and tied timestamp. Competency probe: Distinguish implicit affinity from explicit conversion and test malformed or lossy values. A defensible solution applies Type Conversion, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a typed ticket projection query pack.

content.code:
-- Worked focus: sql-type-conversion — Distinguish implicit affinity from explicit conversion and test malformed or lossy values.
SELECT ticket_id,
       COALESCE(CAST(owner_id AS TEXT), 'unassigned') AS owner,
       CASE severity WHEN 'critical' THEN 1 WHEN 'high' THEN 2 ELSE 3 END AS severity_order
FROM tickets
WHERE resolved_at IS NULL
ORDER BY severity_order, opened_at, ticket_id
LIMIT 6;
content.caption: Runnable relational query or data-system contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: The database always converts text to numbers safely. This fails the Type Conversion contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build Type Conversion evidence · Theory Type Conversion
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable SQLite increment headed "-- Evidence: sql-type-conversion-8wmvsq" with a concrete grain, hypothesis, or prediction; projection, expression, type, alias, or duplicate evidence; and an observation, reconciliation, or verification tied to returned rows, changed rows, an error, or a plan.

Evidence target: Build Type Conversion evidence · Theory Type Conversion.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Type Conversion into observable performance evidence. For Build Type Conversion evidence · Theory Type Conversion, keep Type Conversion connected to executable relational evidence.

content.code:
-- Evidence: sql-type-conversion-8wmvsq
-- grain: declare the exact row or mutation grain before running
SELECT ticket_id,
       CASE severity WHEN 'critical' THEN 1 WHEN 'high' THEN 2 ELSE 3 END AS urgency
FROM tickets
ORDER BY urgency, ticket_id;
-- observation: reconcile the returned rows, changed count, error, or plan with the prediction
